entries.title: Drone won't arm
symptoms: No response when arm switch toggled
causes: Throttle not at zero / minimum
Accelerometer not calibrated
Angle too steep (drone not level)
RX not bound or no signal
USB cable still connected
CLI 'status' shows arming prevention flags
diagnostics: Check Betaflight OSD or Configurator for arming disabled flags
Run 'status' in CLI — look for 'Arming disabled flags'
Verify throttle channel reads 1000 at idle in Receiver tab
Check accelerometer calibration in Setup tab
fixes: Lower throttle stick fully before arming
Calibrate accelerometer: place quad on flat surface → Setup tab → Calibrate Accelerometer
Bind receiver to transmitter
Disconnect USB before attempting to arm
Set arm switch on a dedicated AUX channel in Modes tab

entries.title: Arming but motors don't spin
causes: Motor protocol mismatch (DSHOT vs PWM)
ESC not receiving signal — bad solder joint on signal wire
Motor output pins damaged on FC
DSHOT setting wrong (e.g. DSHOT600 on slow ESC)
diagnostics: Go to Motors tab → move sliders manually (props off!) — do motors spin?
If sliders work but arming doesn't: check Modes tab arm switch config
If sliders don't work: check ESC protocol in Configuration tab
Measure motor signal pad voltage with multimeter: should toggle between 0.2-0.4V on DSHOT
fixes: Match ESC protocol: Configuration → ESC/Motor Features → Motor Protocol
Try DSHOT300 if DSHOT600 doesn't work
Reflow solder joints on motor signal pads
Check motor resource mapping: CLI 'resource' command

entries.title: Motor stuttering / not spinning smoothly
symptoms: Motor twitches but won't spin up
causes: Bad solder joint on motor wires to ESC
Bridged motor pads on ESC
Motor bell debris / bent shaft
Motor screws too long — touching windings
Damaged MOSFET on ESC
Motor wire insulation rubbing on bell causing short
diagnostics: Swap motor to different ESC pad — if motor works, ESC is bad
Swap known-good motor to suspect ESC pad — if new motor fails, ESC is bad
Spin motor by hand — should spin freely with slight magnetic cogging
Check motor screws are not protruding through motor base
Inspect motor wires for wear where they exit the arm
fixes: Reflow all solder joints on that motor's arm
Check and trim motor screws if too long
Remove motor bell and clean debris
Replace damaged MOSFET (advanced) or replace ESC
Re-route motor wires away from bell contact

entries.title: Motor hot after flight
symptoms: Motor too hot to touch after landing
causes: Props too aggressive for motor KV
PID tune too aggressive (D-term oscillation)
Motor bearings failing
Flying on too many cells for the motor rating
Bent motor shaft causing vibration
diagnostics: Compare motor temps — one hotter than others = individual motor problem
All motors hot equally = tune/prop issue
Check blackbox log for D-term oscillation
Spin motors by hand — grinding = bad bearings
fixes: Use lighter / less aggressive props
Lower D-term gains in PID tune
Replace motor bearings or entire motor
Drop cell count (e.g. 6S to 4S) or lower motor KV
Enable motor temp monitoring if ESC supports telemetry

entries.title: ESC desync / death roll
symptoms: Audible motor stutter during hard maneuvers
100% motor output in blackbox on one motor
causes: ESC timing out of sync with motor
Motor KV too high for ESC timing settings
CPU overload on FC causing DSHOT timing errors
Ramp-up power set too high in BLHeli
Low quality ESC unable to handle current spikes
diagnostics: Enable blackbox logging — look for motor hitting 100% while others normal
Check RPM errors in Motors tab (bidirectional DSHOT required)
Check CPU load in CLI: 'status' — should be under 50%
Reproduce: usually happens on hard maneuvers or punch-outs
fixes: BLHeli: set Ramp-Up Power to 0.125 (BLHeli_S) or 12% (BLHeli_32/AM32)
BLHeli: increase motor timing from Low to Medium or Med-High
Betaflight: lower PID loop frequency (8k/4k → 4k/4k)
Betaflight: switch from DSHOT600 to DSHOT300
Betaflight: set Demag Compensation to High
Add capacitor (25V 1000uF low-ESR) to battery pads
Update ESC firmware to latest BLHeli_32 or AM32

entries.title: ESC magic smoke / burned ESC
causes: Voltage spike from motor braking (no capacitor)
Short circuit from crashed prop hitting wires
Water/moisture ingress
Overcurrent — props too heavy for ESC amp rating
Reversed battery polarity
diagnostics: Visual inspection for scorch marks, popped caps, damaged MOSFETs
Continuity test between VBAT+ and motor pads — continuity = blown MOSFET
Smell test — burned electronics have distinct odor
Check if BLHeli Configurator still detects the ESC
fixes: Replace ESC (4-in-1) or affected arm ESC (individual)
ALWAYS add capacitor to prevent future voltage spikes (25V 1000uF+)
Ensure ESC amp rating exceeds max motor draw by 20%+ margin
Conformal coat ESC for moisture protection
Double-check polarity before connecting battery

entries.title: Motors spin on connect / won't calibrate
symptoms: Motors spin immediately when battery connected
causes: ESC not receiving valid throttle signal
Wrong ESC protocol selected
FC motor output stuck high
ESC firmware corrupted
diagnostics: Listen to startup tones: 3 beeps = power OK. 2 long beeps = signal OK
No second set of beeps = ESC not getting valid signal from FC
Check Configuration tab → ESC protocol matches actual ESC type
fixes: Flash ESC firmware via BLHeli Configurator / Bluejay Configurator
Match protocol in Betaflight to ESC capability
Re-solder signal wire from FC to ESC
If individual ESC: try different motor output on FC

entries.title: Drone flips on takeoff
causes: Wrong motor order in Betaflight
Wrong motor spin direction
Props on wrong motors (CW/CCW swapped)
Gyro orientation incorrect
FC mounted in wrong orientation without board alignment set
diagnostics: Motors tab: verify motor 1-4 match Betaflight diagram
Props off — check each motor direction matches Betaflight diagram
Move quad by hand — verify Betaflight 3D model moves correctly
If model moves wrong: FC orientation or board alignment is off
fixes: Remap motors in Motors tab or CLI 'resource motor'
Reverse motor direction in BLHeli or swap any 2 motor wires
Place CW/CCW props on correct motors per Betaflight diagram
Set Board Alignment in Configuration tab to match FC mounting angle
If FC is rotated 90°/180°/270°: set yaw offset accordingly

entries.title: Betaflight won't connect / DFU mode
causes: Wrong USB driver (need STM32 VCP driver or Zadig for DFU)
Damaged USB connector on FC
FC bricked from bad firmware flash
Wrong COM port selected
USB cable is charge-only (no data lines)
diagnostics: Check Device Manager for unknown USB device
Try different USB cable — many cables are charge-only
Hold boot button while connecting USB to force DFU mode
Check if FC appears in DFU mode in Zadig
fixes: Install STM32 Virtual COM Port driver
Use Zadig to install WinUSB driver for DFU mode
Use known-good data USB cable
Flash firmware via DFU: Betaflight Configurator → Firmware Flasher → DFU
If USB port damaged: solder direct to USB pads or use alternate UART with FTDI adapter

entries.title: Flight controller rebooting mid-flight
causes: Voltage regulator on FC overloaded or failing
Loose battery connector causing power interruption
Solder joint on VBAT pad cracked
Electrical noise from ESC/motors causing brownout
Capacitor missing or failed
diagnostics: Check blackbox for power interruptions (voltage drops to 0)
Wiggle battery connector while connected — does FC reboot?
Inspect all power solder joints under magnification
Measure voltage on 5V and 3.3V regulators during motor spin-up
fixes: Reflow VBAT solder joints
Replace battery lead / XT60 connector
Add low-ESR capacitor (25V 1000uF+) across battery pads
If voltage regulator failed: replace FC or use external 5V BEC
Secure battery with anti-slip pad to prevent connector loosening

entries.title: Gyro drift / toilet bowl effect
symptoms: Drone slowly rotates on yaw without input
Heading slowly changes on its own
causes: GPS rescue or position hold interfering (if GPS present)
Magnetometer (compass) not calibrated or interfered by electronics
I-term windup on yaw axis
Vibrations causing gyro noise
Gyro hardware failing
diagnostics: Fly without GPS features enabled — if fixed, GPS/mag issue
Check gyro traces in blackbox — clean or noisy?
Check if toilet bowl only in GPS modes or all modes
Verify FC is soft-mounted to reduce vibrations
fixes: Disable magnetometer if not needed (CLI: 'set mag_hardware = NONE')
Recalibrate compass away from metal/electronics
Soft-mount FC with rubber grommets
Reduce yaw I-term if excessive
Update Betaflight firmware — newer versions have better gyro filtering

entries.title: No FPV video / black screen in goggles
causes: VTX not powered (check voltage input)
Wrong channel/band on VTX vs goggles
Camera not connected or dead
Antenna not connected to VTX — NEVER power VTX without antenna
Goggles antenna connector loose
diagnostics: Is VTX LED on? If not, power issue
Try channel scan on goggles — find any signal?
Connect camera directly to a monitor via composite if possible
Check VTX wiring: VBAT/5V in, video signal from camera, ground
fixes: Match VTX and goggle to same band/channel (e.g. R1 = Raceband Channel 1 = 5658 MHz)
Verify VTX is getting power — measure voltage at VTX input pad
Re-seat camera connector or re-solder camera signal wire
ALWAYS connect antenna before powering VTX
Try different VTX channel — may be on wrong one after firmware flash

entries.title: Video static / lines / interference
causes: Electrical noise from ESCs/motors coupling into video
Missing or inadequate capacitor on battery input
Ground loop — FPV system not sharing common ground with FC
Camera power not filtered (needs LC filter)
VTX too close to ESC / high-current wires
diagnostics: Does noise change with throttle? If yes, motor/ESC noise
Does noise appear with motors off but battery connected? If yes, switching regulator noise
Try powering camera from separate regulated 5V source
fixes: Add capacitor (25V 1000uF low-ESR) across battery pads
Ensure all grounds connected (FC, ESC, VTX, camera share ground)
Add LC filter to camera power input
Route video signal wire away from motor/ESC wires
Use shielded camera cable if available
Twist camera signal and ground wires together to reduce noise pickup

entries.title: FPV video range poor / breaks up close
symptoms: Video breaks up at short range
causes: Damaged or wrong polarization antenna
VTX power set too low (25mW pit mode still active)
Antenna connector (MMCX/UFL) not fully seated
Multipathing / reflections in environment
Goggles antenna damaged
diagnostics: Check VTX power setting — may be stuck in 25mW pit mode
Wiggle VTX antenna connector — any change?
Try known-good antenna
Test in open field vs urban — significantly better = multipath issue
fixes: Increase VTX power: Betaflight OSD → VTX settings, or SmartAudio/Tramp menu
Use matched circular polarized antennas (RHCP on both VTX and goggles)
Re-seat MMCX/UFL connector — they work loose from crashes
Upgrade to higher-gain goggle antennas (patch + omni combo)
Position VTX antenna away from carbon fiber frame (CF blocks RF)

entries.title: Receiver not binding / no link
symptoms: RX LED solid/off (not blinking bind pattern)
Betaflight receiver tab shows no channel movement
causes: RX and TX on different protocols (ELRS vs CRSF vs SBUS)
RX firmware version doesn't match TX firmware
Binding phrase mismatch (ELRS)
RX wired to wrong UART or wrong pads (TX/RX swapped)
Serial RX not enabled on correct port
diagnostics: RX LED behavior: fast blink = searching. Solid = bound. Off = no power
Betaflight Ports tab: is Serial RX enabled on the UART the RX is connected to?
Betaflight Receiver tab: is protocol set correctly (CRSF for ELRS/TBS, SBUS for FrSky)?
For ELRS: are TX and RX on same firmware version? Check via ExpressLRS Configurator
fixes: ELRS: ensure binding phrase matches on TX and RX (set in ExpressLRS Configurator)
ELRS: update both TX and RX to same firmware version
Check wiring: TX pad on FC goes to RX pin on receiver and vice versa
Enable Serial RX on correct UART in Betaflight Ports tab
Set Receiver tab → Serial Receiver Provider to match your RX protocol

entries.title: Failsafe / signal loss mid-flight
symptoms: Drone drops or RTH activates unexpectedly
causes: RX antenna damaged or positioned inside carbon fiber frame
RX antenna too close to VTX antenna (same polarization interference)
Loose UART connection (intermittent signal)
TX power set too low
Too many obstacles / out of range
diagnostics: Check RSSI/LQ in OSD or blackbox — gradual decline = range. Sudden drop = hardware
Inspect RX antenna for cuts/breaks in active element
Wiggle UART wires while monitoring receiver tab — any dropouts?
Compare signal at same distance with antenna inside vs outside frame
fixes: Mount RX antenna outside/away from carbon fiber frame
Use antenna tube/mount to position antenna optimally
Separate RX and VTX antennas (different polarization or physical distance)
Reflow UART solder joints
Increase TX power on radio module
Configure proper failsafe in Betaflight (drop, land, or GPS rescue)

entries.title: No GPS fix / slow fix
causes: GPS module not wired correctly (TX/RX/5V/GND)
GPS UART not configured in Betaflight Ports tab
GPS protocol mismatch (NMEA vs UBLOX)
GPS antenna facing down / blocked by battery
Flying indoors — GPS requires sky view
diagnostics: Does GPS module LED blink? (usually blinks on fix)
Betaflight GPS tab: does it show any data?
Check Ports tab: is GPS enabled on correct UART?
Configuration tab: GPS protocol set to UBLOX (most common)?
fixes: Wire GPS: TX→RX, RX→TX on a free UART. Power from 5V pad
Enable GPS on correct UART in Ports tab
Set GPS protocol to UBLOX in Configuration tab
Mount GPS on top of quad with antenna facing up (away from electronics)
For first use outdoors: allow 5-10 min cold start for almanac download

entries.title: Battery voltage sag / early low voltage
causes: Battery internal resistance too high (old/damaged cells)
Battery C-rating too low for quad's current draw
Unbalanced cells — one cell sagging faster
Flying too aggressively for battery capacity
diagnostics: Check individual cell voltages after flight (balance port)
Measure internal resistance per cell (good: <15mΩ, bad: >30mΩ)
Compare flight time: new battery vs suspect battery
Check OSD voltage under load vs at rest
fixes: Replace battery if IR is high or cells unbalanced by >0.1V
Use higher C-rating battery
Use higher capacity battery (but heavier)
Storage charge batteries when not in use (3.8V/cell)
Never discharge below 3.5V/cell under load

entries.title: Nothing powers on / dead quad
causes: Blown fuse (if FC has TVS diode / fuse)
Reversed polarity connection (check XT60 orientation)
Broken wire in battery lead
Bad solder joint on main power pads
XT60 connector pins pushed back
diagnostics: Measure voltage at battery pads on FC with multimeter — should read battery voltage
If voltage at pads: FC power regulator is dead
If no voltage at pads: wiring issue between battery and FC
Check battery is actually charged (connect to charger/checker)
fixes: Continuity test from battery connector through to FC pads
Reflow main power solder joints
Check for pushed-back pins in XT60
Replace blown TVS diode or trace the power path
If FC regulator dead: use external 5V BEC or replace FC

entries.title: Oscillation / vibrations in flight
causes: PID gains too high (especially D-term)
Gyro filtering insufficient
Bent prop or motor shaft causing vibrations
FC not soft-mounted
Prop balance off
diagnostics: Blackbox: look for D-term oscillation (high-frequency noise on D trace)
Lower D-term by 20% — does oscillation reduce?
Try new/balanced props — does jello disappear?
Check all motors spin freely without vibration
fixes: Lower D-term gains (start at 80% of default)
Apply RPM filtering if ESC supports bidirectional DSHOT
Use Betaflight's gyro filter sliders (increase filtering)
Soft-mount FC with rubber grommets or O-rings
Balance props or use pre-balanced props (e.g. HQProp)
Use Betaflight 4.4+ preset tune as starting point

entries.title: Arm cracked / frame damage after crash
causes: Impact damage from crash
Carbon fiber fatigued from repeated crashes
Motor bolts too tight causing stress fracture
Design weakness in arm/body junction
diagnostics: Visual inspection — flex each arm looking for cracks
Spin props — wobble visible on one arm?
Check motor mount bolts are tight but not overtightened
Hold arm and wiggle — any play at body junction?
fixes: Replace cracked arm (most frames sell individual arms)
Temporary: super glue (CA) + baking soda for field repair
If body-arm junction: may need full bottom plate replacement
Consider upgrading to thicker arms (5mm+) or deadcat geometry

entries.title: Settings lost after firmware update
symptoms: All settings reset to default after BF update
causes: Normal behavior — major Betaflight versions reset settings
Didn't save backup before flashing
Full chip erase was selected during flash
diagnostics: Check if diff/dump backup exists
Major version jump (e.g. 4.3→4.4) always requires reconfiguration
fixes: ALWAYS backup before updating: CLI → 'diff all' → save to file
After flash: CLI → paste your saved diff (review each line for deprecated commands)
Use Betaflight Presets for quick baseline configuration
For minor updates (4.4.1→4.4.2): settings usually preserved if 'no full chip erase'

entries.title: Barometer / altitude reading erratic
causes: Barometer exposed to prop wash / airflow
Barometer not covered (needs light shield + foam)
Temperature changes affecting pressure reading
diagnostics: Cover baro sensor with dark foam — does reading stabilize?
Compare indoor vs outdoor readings
Check Betaflight sensor tab for baro data
fixes: Cover barometer chip with small piece of open-cell foam
Ensure foam is dark (light can affect some baro chips)
If not using altitude hold: disable baro in Betaflight (saves CPU)
For precision altitude: use GPS altitude instead of baro

entries.title: Post-crash inspection checklist
symptoms: Just crashed — what to check before flying again
causes: Crash impact
diagnostics: 1. Props: check for cracks, chips, bent blades. Replace if any damage
2. Motors: spin each by hand. Gritty/grinding = damaged bearing or bent bell
3. Frame: flex each arm. Check for cracks at motor mount and body junction
4. Camera: check mount and angle. Lens cracked?
5. Antenna: VTX antenna still attached? UFL connectors pop off in crashes
6. Battery: check for puffing/swelling. Smell for chemical odor. If puffy, retire immediately
7. Solder joints: visual check for cracked joints (especially XT60, motor wires)
8. Betaflight: connect and check gyro/accelerometer readings. Calibrate accel if needed
fixes: Replace any damaged props — even small chips affect balance
If motor gritty: replace bearings or motor
Reflow any suspect solder joints
Recalibrate accelerometer after crash
Test arm and briefly hover before full send

entries.title: ArduPilot pre-arm check failures
symptoms: Mission Planner shows pre-arm check failed
causes: GPS not locked (need 3D fix with HDOP < 2.0)
Compass not calibrated or inconsistent with GPS heading
Accelerometer not calibrated
Battery failsafe voltage set higher than current battery
RC calibration not performed
EKF variance too high (vibration or sensor disagreement)
diagnostics: Read the exact PreArm message on HUD or in Messages tab
Mission Planner → Flight Data → Messages tab shows specific failure
Check GPS status: need 3D lock, HDOP < 2.0, >6 satellites for Loiter
Check compass health: Mission Planner → Setup → Compass → offsets should be < 600
fixes: GPS: wait for 3D fix outdoors. Cold start can take 5-10 min
Compass: redo calibration away from metal. Offsets > 600 = bad placement
Accel: redo calibration on flat surface via Mission Planner → Setup
EKF: reduce vibrations (soft mount FC, balance props)
To bypass in emergency: set ARMING_CHECK = 0 (not recommended for normal flight)
BRD_SAFETYENABLE = 0 to disable safety switch requirement

entries.title: ArduPilot compass/EKF errors
causes: Compass too close to power wires or ESCs (magnetic interference)
Multiple compasses disagreeing (internal vs external)
Compass calibration done near metal objects
Motor magnetic interference not compensated
diagnostics: Check compass offsets: > 600 on any axis = bad location
Flight log: compare compass heading vs GPS ground course — divergence = interference
MAG_ENABLE compass order: external should be primary
Test: rotate drone 360° in Mission Planner → compass should track smoothly
fixes: Mount external compass on GPS mast ABOVE frame, away from wires
Disable internal compass if external available: COMPASS_USE2 = 0
Redo compass calibration outdoors away from cars/buildings/metal
Enable compass-motor calibration (MOT_COMP_TYPE = 2) for motor interference
Set COMPASS_ORIENT correctly for external compass mounting direction

entries.title: ArduPilot EKF failsafe / flight mode change rejected
symptoms: Drone switches to Land or AltHold unexpectedly
causes: EKF position estimate unreliable (vibrations or sensor issues)
GPS glitch or loss of lock
Barometer affected by airflow (not covered)
IMU clipping from excessive vibrations
diagnostics: Download and review .bin flight log
Check Vibe message: clipping events should be 0. Any clipping = vibration problem
Check EKF status flags in log for innovation failures
Check GPS log: satellite count drops or HDOP spikes
fixes: Soft-mount flight controller to reduce vibrations
Balance propellers (or use pre-balanced)
Cover barometer with open-cell foam
Ensure GPS has clear sky view (not blocked by battery)
Consider setting EKF_CHECK_THRESH slightly higher if marginal
Add GPS mast to raise GPS/compass above frame interference

entries.title: iNav navigation modes not working
causes: GPS fix quality poor (need 3D fix, >6 sats)
Compass not calibrated or interfered
iNav navigation settings not configured
Accelerometer not calibrated on level surface
Board alignment incorrect
diagnostics: iNav Configurator → GPS tab: verify 3D fix with >6 sats
Check compass alignment: rotate drone, heading in configurator should match
Verify nav_extra_arming_safety is allowing arm
Check nav_mc_pos_xy_p and nav_mc_pos_z_p aren't zeroed
fixes: Calibrate compass: iNav Configurator → Calibration → Compass calibration
Set correct board alignment in Configuration tab
Calibrate accelerometer on level surface
Ensure GPS TX/RX wired to correct UART, GPS protocol set correctly
For first-time setup: use iNav's 'Navigation' preset as baseline
Set nav_user_control_mode to match your preference (CRUISE vs ATTI)

entries.title: iNav fixed-wing launch / VTOL transition issues
causes: Motor mix / servo configuration incorrect
Launch parameters not set (nav_fw_launch_*)
CG (center of gravity) too far forward or aft
Control surface throws too large or reversed
Transition speed/altitude settings wrong for VTOL
diagnostics: Verify control surface direction: pitch stick forward → elevator down
Check motor direction and thrust direction
Check CG: should be 25-33% of wing chord from leading edge
For VTOL: verify transition_air_speed and transition_min_time
fixes: Set nav_fw_launch_thr for launch power (typically 1700-1800)
Set nav_fw_launch_motor_delay for safe hand-launch clearance
Adjust servo direction/endpoints in iNav Configurator → Servos tab
Ensure nav_fw_cruise_thr is sufficient for level flight
Balance aircraft at correct CG before flight
For VTOL: increase transition altitude and use ANGLE mode during transition

entries.title: PX4 sensor calibration failures
causes: Not holding vehicle still enough during gyro calibration
Not rotating through all 6 orientations during accel calibration
Magnetic interference during compass calibration
Sensor hardware fault
diagnostics: QGroundControl → Sensors page shows specific failure
Check QGC messages for timeout or inconsistency errors
Try calibration outdoors away from metal/concrete reinforcing
fixes: Gyro: place on flat surface, don't touch for 10 seconds
Accel: follow QGC prompts for each orientation — hold steady 3 seconds each
Compass: calibrate outdoors. Rotate smoothly, not jerky
If persistent failure: check SYS_HAS_MAG, SYS_HAS_BARO parameters
Try power cycling and recalibrating from scratch

entries.title: PX4 failsafe cascade / unexpected landing
symptoms: Mode switches to Return/Land without input
causes: RC signal loss triggering COM_RC_LOSS_T timeout
Data link loss (telemetry radio)
Low battery failsafe (BAT_LOW_THR / BAT_CRIT_THR)
Geofence violation
Mission completion action set to Land
diagnostics: Download ULog flight log from SD card
Check QGC messages for specific failsafe trigger
Review COM_RC_LOSS_T, NAV_RCL_ACT, NAV_DLL_ACT parameters
Check battery cell voltages for imbalance/sag
fixes: Set appropriate RC loss timeout: COM_RC_LOSS_T (default 0.5s may be too aggressive)
Configure NAV_RCL_ACT to desired action (0=disabled, 1=loiter, 2=return, 3=land)
Set battery thresholds appropriately for your pack capacity
Verify geofence boundaries if GF_ACTION is set
For telemetry loss: set NAV_DLL_ACT appropriately

entries.title: ELRS 3.x / 4.x binding issues
symptoms: ELRS receiver won't bind after firmware update
LED pattern shows WiFi mode not bind mode
Binding phrase doesn't work after cross-version flash
causes: TX and RX on different major ELRS versions (3.x vs 4.x incompatible)
Binding phrase set on one but not other
WiFi mode entered instead of bind mode
Regulatory domain mismatch (EU vs FCC vs AU)
diagnostics: Check ELRS version on TX module (ELRS Lua script on radio)
Check ELRS version on RX (WiFi page at 10.0.0.1)
LED blink pattern: slow blink = binding, fast blink = no connection, solid = connected
Double-check regulatory domain matches between TX and RX
fixes: Flash TX and RX to SAME version using ExpressLRS Configurator
Set IDENTICAL binding phrase on both TX and RX firmware builds
To enter bind mode: power on RX, wait 60s for auto-WiFi, or triple-press button if available
For version mismatch: flash RX via WiFi (connect to ELRS_RX hotspot, navigate to 10.0.0.1)
Ensure regulatory domain (ISM band) matches between TX/RX

entries.title: DJI FPV system latency / OSD issues
symptoms: OSD layout wrong after BF update
Custom OSD not displaying in DJI goggles
causes: Betaflight OSD font not updated for DJI compatibility
MSP DisplayPort not configured (for custom OSD)
DJI air unit needs firmware update
UART baud rate mismatch between FC and DJI air unit
diagnostics: Check if analog OSD works (connect analog camera) — if yes, MSP issue
Verify UART config: DJI TX goes to FC RX on a free UART
Check Betaflight Ports tab: is MSP enabled on DJI UART?
Update DJI air unit firmware via DJI Assistant 2
fixes: Enable MSP on the UART connected to DJI air unit (Ports tab → MSP toggle)
Set display_port_msp_speed = 115200 in CLI (Betaflight 4.4+)
Flash correct OSD font: use INAV or BF font flasher for DJI
For custom OSD: WTFOS or similar custom firmware on DJI goggles
For latency: ensure DJI is in LOW LATENCY mode (not High Quality)

entries.title: HDZero / Walksnail no video or poor signal
symptoms: Walksnail breaks up at close range
causes: VTX and goggles on different channels (not auto-matching)
VTX power set to minimum (25mW)
Antenna mismatch (LHCP vs RHCP or linear vs circular)
Firmware version mismatch between goggles and VTX
UFL antenna connector not fully seated after crash
diagnostics: HDZero: check channel in VTX menu via Betaflight OSD
Walksnail: check Avatar HD settings app on goggles
Wiggle UFL connector gently — any change in signal?
Check VTX power setting is appropriate (200-400mW for flying)
fixes: Match channel/band between VTX and goggles
Increase VTX power from pit mode to flight power
Re-seat UFL connector firmly (use antenna wrench if available)
Use matched antennas: same polarization on both ends
Update firmware on both goggles and VTX to latest matching version

entries.title: LiPo battery puffing / swelling
symptoms: Battery won't fit in strap anymore
Reduced flight time with puffed pack
causes: Over-discharged below safe voltage (< 3.3V/cell)
Overcharged above 4.2V/cell
Crash damage to cell (internal short forming)
Charged/stored in extreme heat
Old age — natural cell degradation
diagnostics: Visual: any swelling = gas generation inside cell = damage
Measure cell voltages: any cell > 0.1V different from others = imbalanced
Measure internal resistance: > 30mΩ per cell = degraded
Slight puff after hard flight that goes away = OK (temporary gas). Permanent puff = retire
fixes: Mildly puffed (< 3mm): can fly at reduced capacity but monitor closely
Significantly puffed: RETIRE IMMEDIATELY. Discharge to storage voltage via charger
Disposal: discharge fully, tape terminals, take to battery recycling
NEVER puncture, crush, or throw in fire
Prevention: storage charge (3.8V/cell) when not flying for > 2 days
Prevention: never discharge below 3.5V/cell under load (set OSD warning)

entries.title: Betaflight blackbox logging not working
symptoms: No blackbox log files on SD card
SD card not detected by FC
causes: SD card not formatted correctly (needs FAT32)
SD card too large (some FCs don't support > 32GB)
Blackbox not enabled in Betaflight
SD card slot contacts dirty/damaged
SD card full
diagnostics: CLI: 'blackbox' command — shows logging status
Check Configuration tab → Blackbox logging toggle
Remove SD card, format as FAT32 on computer, reinsert
Try different SD card (use quality brand, Class 10+)
fixes: Enable blackbox in Configuration tab
Format SD card as FAT32 (not exFAT) — max 32GB
Set blackbox logging rate (recommended: 500Hz for 8K PID loop)
Use onboard flash if no SD slot: set blackbox_device = SPIFLASH in CLI
Clean SD card contacts with isopropyl alcohol if intermittent

entries.title: Propwash oscillation on descent
symptoms: Oscillation / wobble when descending through own prop wash
Visible shake in video on dive pullouts
Worse in sharp turns and quick stops
causes: Propellers re-entering their own disturbed air vortex
PID controller unable to compensate fast enough for turbulent airflow
D-term filtering too aggressive (delays response)
Low motor authority at low throttle (idle speed too low)
diagnostics: Reproducible: do a punch-out, then cut throttle and descend — wobble on descent
Worse at specific throttle range (usually 30-50%)
Blackbox: look at gyro traces during descent — oscillation visible
fixes: Increase motor idle speed: set dshot_idle_value slightly higher (6-8%)
Increase D-term (counterintuitive but helps track through turbulence)
Reduce D-term lowpass filtering (allow faster D response)
Enable dynamic idle in Betaflight 4.3+ (adjusts idle per motor RPM)
Use RPM filtering to allow lower overall filtering
Fly through propwash rather than hovering in it (technique)

entries.title: GPS rescue / RTH flyaway
symptoms: GPS rescue sends drone wrong direction
RTH altitude too low — crashes into obstacles
Drone flies away instead of returning
causes: GPS home point set before good fix (inaccurate home)
Compass interference causing wrong heading
GPS rescue altitude set too low for terrain
Wind stronger than drone can fight at rescue throttle
GPS rescue not tested before needed
diagnostics: Check home point location: was GPS fix solid at arm time? (>8 sats, HDOP <2)
Did compass heading agree with GPS heading before takeoff?
Check GPS_RESCUE_MIN_SATS (Betaflight) or GPS_MIN_SATS (ArduPilot)
fixes: Always wait for solid GPS fix before arming (>10 sats preferred)
Set GPS rescue altitude above all nearby obstacles + 20m margin
Test GPS rescue in safe area at low altitude first
Betaflight: configure GPS_RESCUE_* parameters properly
ArduPilot: set RTL_ALT to safe altitude (in cm — e.g. 5000 = 50m)
Set minimum satellite requirement: don't arm without >8 sats in GPS modes

entries.title: Motor doesn't spin in one direction (reversed)
symptoms: Betaflight motor direction doesn't match diagram
causes: Motor wired with swapped phase wires
BLHeli direction setting wrong
diagnostics: Motors tab: spin each motor and verify direction matches Betaflight diagram
Props off — verify visually which direction each motor spins
fixes: BLHeli Suite/Configurator: change motor direction in ESC settings (easiest)
Hardware: swap any 2 of the 3 motor wires to ESC
Betaflight 4.4+: can reverse motor direction per-motor in Motors tab

entries.title: Motor makes grinding noise
causes: Bent motor shaft from crash
Bearing damaged — sand/dirt ingress
Motor bell rubbing on stator
Debris (grass/hair) wound around shaft
diagnostics: Remove prop and spin motor by hand — should be smooth with light magnetic cogging
Remove motor bell: inspect for debris, shaft straightness, bearing play
Compare sound to known-good motor
fixes: Clean debris from around shaft and bell gap
Replace bearings (press out old, press in new — same bore size)
If shaft bent: replace motor (shaft press not worth it on mini motors)
Preventive: use motor wire boots and conformal coat

entries.title: BLHeli Suite won't detect ESCs
causes: Wrong ESC protocol selected in Betaflight (need DSHOT for BLHeli passthrough)
USB cable issue (charge-only cable)
FC firmware doesn't support 4-way interface
ESC firmware corrupted
diagnostics: Ensure battery is connected (BLHeli needs ESC power, not just USB)
Betaflight motors tab: do motors spin with sliders? If yes, signal path is OK
Try BLHeli Configurator (Chrome app) vs BLHeli Suite (standalone) — different compatibility
fixes: Connect battery + USB simultaneously
Use DSHOT protocol in Betaflight Configuration tab
For BLHeli_S: use BLHeli Configurator or Bluejay Configurator
For BLHeli_32: use BLHeli Suite
For AM32: use AM32 Configurator
If all fails: flash ESC firmware via signal pad using Arduino as programmer

entries.title: DJI/HDZero/Walksnail no link
symptoms: Digital FPV shows 'no link' or searching
causes: VTX and goggles not paired / on different channels
Firmware mismatch between VTX and goggles
VTX antenna not connected (UFL popped off in crash)
VTX damaged from powering on without antenna
diagnostics: Check VTX LED status: solid=linked, blinking=searching
DJI: long-press link button on both goggles and VTX simultaneously
HDZero: check VTX firmware matches goggle firmware version
Inspect UFL/MMCX connector under magnification
fixes: Re-pair: hold link buttons on VTX and goggles (varies by system)
Update both VTX and goggles to matching firmware version
Re-seat antenna connector — press firmly until click
If VTX damaged from no-antenna power-on: replace VTX
HDZero: try toggling between 25mW and higher power

entries.title: OSD not showing / garbled OSD
symptoms: No OSD elements on video feed
causes: OSD not enabled in Betaflight
Analog OSD chip on FC not connected to camera/VTX signal path
OSD font not uploaded (garbled characters)
For digital: MSP OSD not configured on correct UART
diagnostics: Betaflight OSD tab: are elements positioned on the screen?
Analog: is video signal routed through FC (camera→FC→VTX) not bypassing it?
Digital: is MSP enabled on the UART connected to VTX in Ports tab?
fixes: Enable OSD elements in Betaflight OSD tab and position them
Upload OSD font: OSD tab → Font Manager → Upload
Analog: wire camera→FC video input, then FC video output→VTX
Digital (DJI/HDZero): enable MSP on VTX UART in Ports tab
Check OSD is not disabled by switch (Modes tab)

entries.title: ELRS won't update / flash fails
causes: Wrong target selected for RX hardware
RX not in bootloader/WiFi mode
USB driver issue (for USB-flashing RXs)
WiFi password incorrect
diagnostics: Check exact RX hardware model — many variants with different MCUs
For WiFi flash: power RX without TX bound for 60s → WiFi AP should appear
For UART flash: hold boot button on RX while powering on
fixes: Verify correct target in ExpressLRS Configurator for your exact RX model
WiFi flash: connect to RX WiFi AP (ExpressLRS RX) → navigate to 10.0.0.1
Betaflight passthrough: set RX UART to 420000 baud, use BF passthrough in ELRS Configurator
If bricked: UART flash with FTDI adapter directly to RX TX/RX pads
ELRS 3.x: use binding phrase instead of button binding

entries.title: LiPo battery puffed/swollen
symptoms: Battery warm when not in use
causes: Over-discharged below 3.0V/cell
Overcharged above 4.25V/cell
Physical damage from crash
Charging immediately after flight while hot
Old age / high cycle count
diagnostics: Visual: any puffing = internal gas from damaged cells
Measure individual cell voltages — if any below 3.0V or significantly unbalanced, battery is damaged
Check internal resistance per cell — high IR confirms degradation
fixes: STOP USING puffy batteries — they are a fire hazard
Dispose safely: discharge to 0V using light bulb method, then recycle at battery drop-off
Store batteries at storage voltage (3.8V/cell) when not in use
Never charge unattended. Use fireproof LiPo bag
Let batteries cool to room temperature before charging
Retire batteries after ~200 cycles or when IR doubles from new

entries.title: Accelerometer won't calibrate / drifts
symptoms: Betaflight model shows quad tilted when level
Angle mode drifts to one side
causes: FC not perfectly level during calibration
FC board physically warped from crash or bad mounting
Accelerometer chip damaged
Board alignment offset needed but not set
diagnostics: Setup tab: place quad on level surface — does 3D model show level?
If tilted: try calibrating again on confirmed flat surface (use spirit level)
If calibration fails: accelerometer chip may be damaged
fixes: Place on verified flat surface → Setup tab → Calibrate Accelerometer
If FC is mounted at angle: set Board Alignment in Configuration tab
If accel chip damaged: disable accel in CLI and fly ACRO only (no angle/horizon mode)
For precision: use CLI 'set acc_trim_roll' and 'set acc_trim_pitch' for fine offset

entries.title: UART conflict / not enough UARTs
symptoms: Can't use GPS + receiver + VTX control simultaneously
Peripheral stops working when another is enabled
Betaflight Ports tab shows limited UARTs
causes: FC has limited UARTs (F411 only has 2 usable)
Two peripherals assigned to same UART
SoftSerial not enabled for additional ports
UART physically damaged
diagnostics: Ports tab: count available UARTs
Check which peripherals need UARTs: RX(1), GPS(1), VTX SmartAudio/Tramp(1), ESC telemetry(1)
CLI 'resource': shows all UART assignments
fixes: Prioritize: RX is mandatory, others are optional
Enable SoftSerial in Configuration tab for 1-2 extra software UARTs
Use SBUS for RX (only needs half-UART), freeing TX pin for other use
Move VTX control to SmartAudio (single-wire, needs only TX)
Upgrade to F405/F722/H743 FC with more UARTs
Use LED pin for SoftSerial if no LED strip needed

entries.title: Prop flies off during flight
symptoms: Find prop on ground after crash
causes: Prop nut not tight enough
Wrong prop nut size (M5 vs T-mount)
Motor shaft threads stripped
Press-fit prop not seated fully
diagnostics: After each session: check prop tightness
Inspect motor shaft threads for wear
Check if prop adapter/collet matches motor shaft
fixes: Use threadlocker (blue Loctite) on prop nuts
Replace stripped motor shaft or motor
Ensure correct prop mount type: M5 thread vs T-Mount vs press-fit
Pre-flight: tug test on every prop before each session

entries.title: Betaflight preset not working as expected
symptoms: Applied preset but quad flies worse
causes: Preset designed for different prop/motor/frame class
Preset overrode your custom rates
Preset assumes specific ESC protocol or features (RPM filter)
Preset from old Betaflight version
diagnostics: Read preset description carefully — what hardware is it for?
Check if preset changed your rates (Rates tab)
Check if preset enabled features your hardware doesn't support (RPM filter needs bidir DSHOT)
fixes: Pick presets matching your exact frame size and motor class
After applying tune preset: reapply your personal rate preset separately
If oscillating: preset may need RPM filter — enable bidirectional DSHOT + Bluejay firmware
Start with a conservative preset (e.g. SupaFly for 5") and adjust from there

entries.title: Cold weather flight issues
symptoms: Dramatically reduced flight time in cold
causes: LiPo internal resistance increases dramatically below 10°C
Cold battery can't deliver rated current
Cold can cause cell imbalance
diagnostics: Check battery temperature before flight — below 15°C is concerning
Compare flight time: warm day vs cold day
fixes: Warm batteries before flight (keep in jacket pocket, car heater, hand warmers)
Never fly LiPo below 5°C battery temperature
Hover briefly before full send to warm the pack under gentle load
Use insulated battery strap or wrap in winter
Consider Li-Ion for cold weather (better cold tolerance than LiPo)

entries.title: Cold solder joint / intermittent connection
symptoms: Component works on bench but fails in flight
Issue appears after vibration or crash
causes: Solder joint not properly heated — cold/dull joint
Solder blob without actual adhesion to pad
Wire strand not tinned before soldering
Pad lifted from PCB
diagnostics: Visual: good joint is shiny and concave. Bad joint is dull, balled up, or cracked
Wiggle each wire while component is powered — does it cut in/out?
Magnifying glass or USB microscope: inspect all joints
fixes: Reflow: apply flux, heat joint until solder flows and wets both surfaces
If pad lifted: scrape trace nearby and solder wire to exposed copper
Tin wire ends before soldering to pads
Use proper temperature (350-380°C for leaded, 380-400°C for lead-free)
Add flux to every joint — flux is the secret to good solder

entries.title: GPS rescue flies wrong direction / RTH fails
symptoms: GPS rescue activates but drone flies away
causes: Compass/magnetometer not calibrated or interfered
GPS arrow orientation wrong in Betaflight
Not enough satellites for reliable fix (need 8+)
GPS rescue parameters not configured
diagnostics: GPS tab: check satellite count (need 8+ for reliable rescue)
Walk with quad: does GPS arrow point correct direction of travel?
Check for magnetic interference: compare heading near electronics vs away
fixes: Calibrate compass outdoors away from metal/electronics
Set correct GPS module orientation in Configuration tab
Configure GPS rescue in Failsafe tab: set altitude, min sats, sanity checks
Mount GPS/compass as far from motors/ESC/battery as possible
Test GPS rescue at low altitude over soft ground first
Set minimum satellite threshold to 8 before GPS rescue arms

entries.title: TX/RX wires swapped — receiver or VTX not communicating
symptoms: Receiver bound but Betaflight shows no channel movement
GPS connected but no data in Betaflight GPS tab
causes: TX pad on FC wired to TX pad on peripheral (should be TX→RX)
RX pad on FC wired to RX pad on peripheral (should be RX→TX)
This is the single most common wiring mistake in FPV
diagnostics: Betaflight Receiver tab: do channels move when you move sticks? If not, TX/RX likely swapped
Check physical wiring: FC TX pad should have wire going to peripheral RX pad
If you can read board silkscreen in photo: verify TX→RX crossing
Try swapping the two signal wires — if it works, they were swapped
fixes: Swap the TX and RX wires: FC TX → peripheral RX, FC RX → peripheral TX
Remember: TX (transmit) sends data OUT, RX (receive) takes data IN
The crossing is correct: what one board transmits, the other receives
Some FCs label pads from the FC's perspective, some from the peripheral's — check the manual

entries.title: Wrong voltage to receiver — 5V to 3.3V device
symptoms: Receiver smells burnt after first power-on
causes: Powered a 3.3V receiver from a 5V pad
Spektrum/DSMX receivers are 3.3V — 5V will kill them
Some ELRS receivers are 3.3V only (check datasheet)
Wire accidentally touching VBAT pad instead of regulated output
diagnostics: Check receiver datasheet for input voltage range
Measure voltage on the power wire with multimeter before connecting receiver
If receiver is dead: check for burn marks on voltage regulator chip
Smell test — burnt electronics have distinct acrid smell
fixes: ALWAYS check receiver voltage requirement before wiring
Use FC's 3V3 pad for 3.3V receivers, 5V/4V5 pad for 5V receivers
If receiver is burnt: replace it — voltage regulators don't survive overvoltage
Add a 3.3V regulator if FC doesn't have a 3V3 pad
Label your power pads with a marker to prevent future mistakes

entries.title: No OSD on digital FPV — MSP not configured
symptoms: DJI/Walksnail/HDZero video works but no OSD overlay
Can see camera image but no battery voltage, RSSI, etc.
OSD elements configured in Betaflight but not showing in goggles
causes: MSP (MSP DisplayPort) not enabled on the UART connected to the digital VTX
TX/RX wires between FC and VTX not connected or swapped
Wrong UART selected in Betaflight Ports tab
diagnostics: Betaflight Ports tab: is 'MSP' enabled (under Peripherals) on the UART your VTX is connected to?
Check which UART the VTX TX/RX wires are soldered to on the FC
For DJI: the VTX needs both TX and RX wires connected for MSP OSD
fixes: Betaflight → Ports tab → find the UART your VTX is on → enable MSP under Peripherals column
Verify wiring: FC TX → VTX RX, FC RX → VTX TX (both wires needed for MSP)
For DJI: also set OSD to 'Auto' or 'MSP DisplayPort' in DJI goggles settings
For HDZero: enable MSP on the correct UART, OSD shows automatically
CLI shortcut: `serial <uart_number> 0 115200 57600 0 115200` to enable MSP on a UART

entries.title: ESC signal wires wrong order — motors spin in wrong positions
symptoms: Drone flips on arm but motor directions are correct
Motors spin but quad is uncontrollable
Motor 1 in Betaflight spins the wrong physical motor
causes: Motor signal wires soldered to wrong M1-M4 pads on FC
ESC ribbon cable upside down or offset by one pin
FC motor resource mapping doesn't match physical wiring
diagnostics: Props OFF — go to Betaflight Motors tab
Spin Motor 1 slider — which physical motor spins? Should match Betaflight diagram
Spin Motor 2, 3, 4 — each should match the Betaflight motor layout diagram
If wrong: note which motor spins for each number
fixes: Option 1 (easiest): Remap motors in Betaflight — Motors tab → drag motors to correct positions
Option 2: CLI `resource motor <number> <pin>` to reassign output pins
Option 3 (hardware): Re-solder signal wires to correct M pads
For 4-in-1 ESC with ribbon: check ribbon orientation matches FC pinout
After fixing: re-test all 4 motors individually before props on

entries.title: Missing ground wire causing erratic behavior
symptoms: ESC desync-like symptoms but ESC is fine
causes: Ground wire not connected between FC and ESC board
Ground wire not connected to receiver
No common ground between VTX and FC
Ground wire broken inside insulation (invisible break)
diagnostics: Check continuity between GND pads on FC, ESC, receiver, and VTX with multimeter
All ground pads should show 0 ohms (continuity) to each other
Wiggle ground wires while powered — does behavior change?
If JST connector: check if ground pin is making contact
fixes: Solder a ground wire between every board in the system — FC, ESC, RX, VTX, GPS all share ground
Even if a JST connector has ground, add a direct solder ground wire as backup
For carbon fiber frames: the frame itself is NOT a reliable ground path
Check JST crimps — ground wire may look connected but have a bad crimp

entries.title: UART conflict — two devices on same port
symptoms: One peripheral works but another stops
GPS works until receiver is powered
Betaflight shows data from wrong device
causes: Two peripherals physically wired to the same UART TX/RX pads
Betaflight Ports tab has two functions enabled on the same UART
SoftSerial conflicting with hardware UART on same timer
diagnostics: Betaflight Ports tab: check each UART row — only ONE function should be enabled per UART
Physically trace wires: are two devices sharing the same TX or RX pad?
CLI: `serial` command shows all UART assignments
fixes: Move one device to a different UART — desolder and rewire to unused TX/RX pads
In Ports tab: ensure each UART has only one peripheral assigned
If out of UARTs: enable SoftSerial for low-bandwidth devices (SmartAudio, GPS)
Consider upgrade to FC with more UARTs (F7/H7 boards have 6-8)
VTX SmartAudio only needs TX wire — can share a UART with a device that only uses RX

entries.title: I am having this same issue
symptoms: My miniquad wont come down after i armed and try to hover, in order to make the quad drop, i need to disarm.
causes: My miniquad wont come down after i armed and try to hover, in order to make the quad drop, i need to disarm.
diagnostics: My miniquad wont come down after i armed and try to hover, in order to make the quad drop, i need to disarm.
fixes: My miniquad wont come down after i armed and try to hover, in order to make the quad drop, i need to disarm.

entries.title: Which FAA pathway for DFR — Part 91 COW vs Part 107?
symptoms: Agency wants to start DFR program — unclear which regulatory pathway to use
Confused between COA, COW, Part 91, Part 107
Old TBVLOS or FR-BVLOS process referenced — is it still valid?
causes: Multiple FAA pathways exist for DFR — Part 91 (PAO/PSO) and Part 107 (civil)
Legacy COA types (TBVLOS, FR-BVLOS) replaced by unified COW framework in 2024-2025
Volunteer/non-government agencies cannot qualify as PAO and must use Part 107
diagnostics: Is the agency a government entity (PD, FD, EMS, government SAR)? → Part 91 COW
Is the agency a volunteer org, 501(c)(3), or non-government? → Part 107 waiver via DroneZone
Does the agency have an existing TBVLOS or FR-BVLOS COA? → Those are valid until expiry but will NOT be renewed; plan COW transition
fixes: Government agencies: Email 9-AVS-AFS-750-91.113Waivers@faa.gov requesting PAO/PSO 91.113 COW packet. State whether you want 200' Shielded Ops or 400' DAA type.
Non-government agencies: Apply via FAA DroneZone (faa.gov/droneZone) for Part 107 BVLOS waiver. Fort Wayne PD received first no-VO Part 107 DFR waiver in 2024.
Start with 200' Shielded Ops COW — 87% of applicants choose this. Faster approval (2-4 weeks), no DAA hardware required.
Do NOT use old CAPS system or DroneZone for Part 91 COW — email only to above address.
COW offers 4-year validity, no NOTAMs, no monthly reporting, airspace authorization included.

entries.title: COW application returned or marked incomplete by FAA
symptoms: FAA email says application requires agency action
COW submission bounced back — status shows incomplete
No response from FAA after submitting waiver package
causes: As of June 2025, 58 of 78 pending COW applications required agency action due to errors
Missing PSO certification letter
Missing or illegible ADS-B In receiver specification
Unsigned or un-initialed checklist sections
Remote ID compliance not verified
Lost-link procedure not documented
diagnostics: FAA Form 7711-2: Is it complete, signed, and dated?
PSO eligibility certification letter: attached?
Self-select checklist: every provision initialed AND signed?
Remote ID compliance: verified at uasdoc.faa.gov and documented?
Anti-collision lighting: specs included showing 3 statute mile visibility?
C2 link: type specified (RF, LTE), lost-link latency threshold, and RTH behavior documented?
ADS-B In receiver: specific model/part number included (not a cell phone or internet service)?
fixes: Verify all 7 checklist items before resubmitting: (1) Form 7711-2 complete+signed, (2) PSO letter attached, (3) checklist initialed+signed, (4) Remote ID verified at uasdoc.faa.gov, (5) anti-collision light specs (3SM), (6) C2 link type + lost-link latency + RTH procedure, (7) ADS-B In receiver model specified.
Write legibly — FAA explicitly flags illegible submissions as incomplete.

For Item 7 (CONOPS): 1-2 paragraph overview is sufficient — full CONOPS document not required. Cover operation type, aircraft description, area, altitude, collision mitigation, C2 link, and crew qualification.
Resubmit to 9-AVS-AFS-750-91.113Waivers@faa.gov with all items addressed.

entries.title: DFR drone flying over people and moving vehicles — when is it legal?
symptoms: Agency dispatching drone on every 911 call including traffic stops and crowds
Unsure if COW covers flying over people and cars
City attorney questioning legality of over-people operations
causes: 91.113 COW permits over-people for life-safety emergencies without special equipment
Routine (non-emergency) over-people ops require additional equipage not covered by base COW
Common applicant mistake: treating every call-out as a life-safety emergency
diagnostics: Is this a genuine life-safety emergency (active threat to life)? → COW permits transitory over-people
Is this routine dispatch (traffic check, noise complaint, patrol support)? → Additional equipment required
Is drone weight 0.88 lbs or less? → Prop guards sufficient for routine over-people
Is drone weight over 0.88 lbs? → PRS (parachute) conforming to ASTM F3322-18 required for routine over-people
Is the operation hovering/loitering over non-participants? → NOT permitted even in emergencies
fixes: For life-safety emergencies: COW permits transitory over-people/vehicles without PRS. Operations must be transitory — no loitering or hovering over non-participants.
For routine over-people ops: equip with (a) Part 107 Subpart D category-compliant drone, OR (b) prop guards if under 0.88 lbs, OR (c) ASTM F3322-18 PRS if over 0.88 lbs.
For large events (parades, sporting events): apply for SGI authorization from FAA SOSC at 9-ator-hq-sosc@faa.gov.
Program policy should define which call types qualify as life-safety vs routine to avoid mission creep (EFF found 1-in-10 CVPD flights lacked stated purpose).
FAA explicit guidance: if agency intends to dispatch on every call, equip with category-compliant drone or PRS.

entries.title: Legacy TBVLOS or FR-BVLOS COA expired — how to renew
symptoms: Agency's TBVLOS or FR-BVLOS COA has expired or is near expiration
FAA rejected renewal application submitted through CAPS
Agency grounded — cannot operate BVLOS without valid authorization
causes: TBVLOS and FR-BVLOS COA types discontinued in 2024-2025
These legacy types replaced by unified 91.113 COW framework
Renewal through old CAPS system no longer accepted for these types
diagnostics: Is it a TBVLOS or FR-BVLOS COA? → Must reapply under new COW framework
Is it a VLOS-only Blanket or Jurisdictional COA? → Still processed via CAPS
Did agency submit renewal to CAPS or DroneZone? → Wrong pathway for BVLOS COA renewal
fixes: Do NOT submit to CAPS or DroneZone for TBVLOS/FR-BVLOS renewal — these are no longer accepted.
Email 9-AVS-AFS-750-91.113Waivers@faa.gov requesting new PAO/PSO 91.113 COW packet.

Apply fresh under new COW framework — this is not a penalty, the new framework offers better capabilities: 4-year validity (vs 2-year), no NOTAMs, no monthly reporting, airspace authorization included.
Existing legacy COAs remain valid until expiration — continue operating under old authorization while new COW application is in review.
New COW approval typically 2-4 weeks. Apply early — don't wait until expiration.

entries.title: Part 108 BVLOS rulemaking — impact on existing COW
symptoms: Agency holding current COW wondering if Part 108 will invalidate it
Vendor recommending major Part 108 prep investments now
Part 107 BVLOS waiver expired — told to wait for Part 108
causes: Part 108 NPRM (FAA-2025-1908, August 7 2025) proposes eliminating new Part 107 BVLOS waivers once final rule takes effect
Final rule target spring 2026; implementation 6-12 months later (late 2026-early 2027)
Industry pushing for grandfathering pathway for existing waiver holders
Part 108 introduces two pathways: Operating Permit (24-month) and Operating Certificate (ongoing)
diagnostics: Does agency have an active COW? → Remains valid during transition period — no action required now
Is agency on Part 107 BVLOS waiver? → Monitor final rule for grandfathering guidance
Is vendor recommending immediate SMS/certificate program investment? → Reasonable for large programs, premature for small agencies
fixes: Existing COWs remain valid during Part 108 interim period — continue operating under current authorization.
Monitor FAA Part 108 docket (FAA-2025-1908) for final rule and transition guidance.
Begin building Part 108 documentation now if scaling program: safety management system (SMS), training program documentation, organizational policies.
Do NOT let Part 107 BVLOS waiver lapse waiting for Part 108 — apply for COW renewal now if needed.
Part 108 Operating Permit pathway (lower complexity, 24-month) likely sufficient for most municipal DFR programs.
Sources: Part 108 NPRM FAA-2025-1908, DLA Piper analysis Oct 2025, EO 14307 June 5 2025.

entries.title: FC UART assignment — TX/RX crossed, no data
entries.symptom: Receiver, GPS, VTX, or ESC telemetry not working despite correct wiring
entries.cause: UART TX and RX are labeled from the FC's perspective, not the peripheral's. FC TX connects to peripheral RX, and vice versa. Most wiring failures are this crossed connection.
entries.fix: Swap TX and RX wires between FC and the device. In Betaflight Ports tab, enable Serial RX on the correct UART number. Verify with `serial` CLI command.

entries.title: ESC signal wire — DSHOT not working, motors won't spin
entries.symptom: Motors don't respond or spin erratically. DSHOT selected in Betaflight but no response.
entries.cause: ESC signal pads require direct connection from FC motor outputs. Common issues: wrong pad, shared ground missing, or bi-directional DSHOT enabled on ESC that doesn't support it.
entries.fix: 1) Connect FC M1–M4 pads directly to ESC signal inputs. 2) Ensure common ground between FC and ESC. 3) In Betaflight Motor tab, set DSHOT300 or DSHOT600. 4) If using bi-dir DSHOT, flash ESC with AM32 or BLHeli_32 firmware that supports it.

entries.title: ELRS receiver wiring — no signal, solid LED
entries.symptom: ELRS receiver powers on (LED solid) but Betaflight shows no RC input. LQ stays at 0.
entries.cause: CRSF protocol requires UART at 420000 baud with half-duplex off. Wrong UART, inverted serial, or wrong protocol selected in Betaflight.
entries.fix: 1) Wire receiver TX→FC RX, receiver RX→FC TX on a free UART. 2) Ports tab: enable Serial RX on that UART. 3) Configuration tab: Receiver Mode = Serial, Provider = CRSF. 4) CLI: `set serialrx_inverted = OFF`, `set serialrx_halfduplex = OFF`, `save`. 5) Bind receiver to transmitter.

entries.title: VTX UART control not working — channel/power won't change
entries.symptom: FPV video works but can't change VTX channel or power from OSD/transmitter.
entries.cause: VTX control via Tramp or SmartAudio requires a dedicated UART TX connection (one-wire). Not connected, or wrong UART, or VTX protocol mismatch.
entries.fix: 1) Connect VTX control wire to any FC TX pad (one-wire, no RX needed). 2) Ports tab: enable VTX (TBS SmartAudio or IRC Tramp) on that UART. 3) In OSD or VTX menu, confirm protocol matches your VTX. 4) Some VTX require TX pad only — don't connect RX.

entries.title: GPS no fix — connected but no satellites
entries.symptom: GPS shows connected in Betaflight/ArduPilot but 0 satellites or never gets fix.
entries.cause: GPS baud rate mismatch, wrong UART, or antenna blocked/indoors. M8N default is 9600 baud; M9N/M10 may auto-negotiate but some configs need manual set.
entries.fix: 1) Ensure GPS is on a dedicated UART with GPS protocol enabled (Ports tab). 2) Set baud to 57600 or 115200 and match in GPS module config (u-center for u-blox). 3) Take outdoors with clear sky view — never test GPS indoors. 4) CLI: `gpspassthrough` to verify communication.

entries.title: Voltage/current sensor not reading — always shows 0V or wrong value
entries.symptom: Battery voltage reads 0 or wildly wrong. Current always 0A.
entries.cause: Voltage and current sensing require correct pad connections and calibration values in Betaflight. Many FCs have dedicated VBAT and CURR pads; wrong scale factors produce wrong readings.
entries.fix: 1) Connect battery + to VBAT pad and ground to GND. 2) In Betaflight Power & Battery tab, set voltage scale (default ~110 for most FCs — adjust until reading matches multimeter). 3) For current: connect ESC current output to FC CURR pad; set current scale to match ESC spec (e.g. 250 for 50A ESC). 4) CLI: `set vbat_scale`, `set current_meter_scale`.

entries.title: Props wash / oscillations on fast direction changes
entries.symptom: Quad shakes or wobbles briefly during quick throttle chops or direction reversals, especially in freestyle moves.
entries.cause: P gains too high, or D term too low to damp P overshoot. Also common with poorly balanced props or high motor noise feeding back into gyro.
entries.fix: 1) Reduce P gains by 10–15% on Roll and Pitch. 2) Increase D gains slightly (5–10 points) to damp oscillations. 3) Check prop balance — even small imbalance amplifies P oscillations. 4) Enable RPM filtering if ESC supports bi-dir DSHOT. 5) Run Betaflight blackbox and look at gyro traces — clean P trace with D tracking = good tune.

entries.title: Quad feels mushy / slow to respond — sluggish handling
entries.symptom: Drone feels soft, doesn't snap to stick inputs, lags noticeably.
entries.cause: P gains too low, I term too high (fighting stick input), or D term too high dampening response.
entries.fix: 1) Increase P gains by 10–20% on Roll/Pitch until you feel response without oscillation. 2) Reduce I term slightly if quad feels like it's fighting you (I resists stick movement). 3) Lower D if it feels like movement is being dragged. 4) Check RC smoothing — too much smoothing kills feel. Set RC smoothing to AUTO or 25ms.

entries.title: Hot motors after flight — possible filter or PID issue
entries.symptom: Motors noticeably hot after a 3–5 minute flight. Getting hotter over time.
entries.cause: D term too high generates high-frequency motor signals (D-term buzz) that heat motors without doing useful work. Also caused by unfiltered high-frequency noise feeding into PID loop.
entries.fix: 1) Reduce D gains by 10–15% and fly again — if motors cooler, D was too high. 2) Enable RPM filtering (requires bi-dir DSHOT + supported ESC firmware) — dramatically reduces D-term buzz. 3) Check motor screws tight, no arm cracks, props balanced. 4) In Betaflight Filtering tab, increase gyro lowpass cutoff slightly if over-filtered (this is counterintuitive but over-filtering can cause noise issues at certain frequencies).

entries.title: Motors spin in wrong direction — quad flips on takeoff
entries.symptom: Quad immediately flips or rolls on takeoff. One or more motors spinning wrong direction.
entries.cause: Brushless motor direction depends on phase wire order. Any two wires swapped reverses direction. Factory direction may not match Betaflight motor layout.
entries.fix: 1) In Betaflight Motor tab, with props OFF, spin each motor and verify direction matches the diagram (standard: M1 CCW, M2 CW, M3 CW, M4 CCW for Betaflight default). 2) To reverse: in Betaflight Motor tab, click reverse on that motor (DSHOT command) — no soldering needed with DSHOT. 3) If no DSHOT: swap any two of the three phase wires on the offending motor.

entries.title: FC won't arm — pre-arming checks failing
entries.symptom: Cannot arm. Betaflight OSD shows flags or beeping. Arm switch has no effect.
entries.cause: Betaflight has pre-arm checks. Common blockers: throttle not at zero, gyro not calibrated, angle too steep, GPS not ready (if required), RX signal lost.
entries.fix: 1) Open Betaflight CLI, type `status` — lists active arming flags. 2) Most common fix: ensure throttle stick is at minimum before arming. 3) `ANGLE` flag: level the quad before arming. 4) `RX LOSS` flag: receiver not connected or not bound. 5) `NOPREARM` flag: configure a prearm switch or disable in CLI: `set use_unsynced_pwm = OFF`. 6) Check Modes tab — arm switch must be assigned.

entries.title: Maiden flight checklist — common pre-flight mistakes
entries.symptom: First flight fails or causes crash — props, direction, FC orientation all need verification.
entries.cause: Build errors that survive bench testing but cause crashes on first flight.
entries.fix: Maiden checklist: 1) Props correct direction (leading edge faces rotation direction). 2) Props tight — hand-tighten then 1/4 turn with tool. 3) Motors correct direction (test in Betaflight motor tab, props OFF). 4) FC board orientation set in Betaflight (Configuration → Board Alignment). 5) Rates not too aggressive for first flight — start with low rates. 6) Arm on bench and tilt — verify quad compensates in correct direction. 7) Test failsafe: arm, fly hover, turn off TX — verify RTH or land behavior. 8) Check CG — should be roughly centered.

entries.title: Video feed has noise / lines when throttle increases
entries.symptom: FPV image clean at idle, gets horizontal lines or noise as throttle rises.
entries.cause: ESC switching noise coupling into video circuit through shared power. Classic ground loop or insufficient filtering on VTX power.
entries.fix: 1) Add LC filter on VTX power supply (available for ~$3, e.g. Matek FCHUB LC filter). 2) Power VTX from filtered 9V or 12V BEC rather than direct battery. 3) Run VTX and camera power/ground separately from ESC power leads. 4) Add capacitor (1000–2200µF 35V) across main battery pads on ESC. 5) Keep VTX antenna away from ESC/motor wires.

entries.title: Frame vibration — oscillations at certain throttle bands
entries.symptom: Quad vibrates noticeably at specific throttle levels. Video shaky at those points. Blackbox shows gyro noise spikes.
entries.cause: Motor/prop resonance hitting a frame natural frequency. Often worsened by loose screws, cracked arms, or unbalanced props.
entries.fix: 1) Check all motor mount screws — even one loose screw dramatically increases vibration. 2) Inspect arms for hairline cracks (common at motor mount and arm-body junction). 3) Balance props with a prop balancer — most significant single fix. 4) Add soft-mount o-rings or grommets under FC to isolate vibration from reaching gyro. 5) In Betaflight, run blackbox and identify the resonance frequency — adjust notch filters to target it.

entries.title: Cracked arm — field repair options
entries.symptom: Arm cracked at motor mount or body junction after crash. Quad flies but unstable, or won't fly.
entries.cause: Carbon fiber arms crack under impact stress, particularly at stress concentrations around motor mount holes and where the arm meets the main body plates.
entries.fix: Field repair (temporary): 1) Wrap crack tightly with electrical tape or fiber tape — not structural but can get you flying. 2) CA glue (super glue) + baking soda accelerator fills small cracks. Permanent fix: replace the arm — most frames use replaceable arms, not monocoque. Order spare arms before you need them. Inspect: tighten all motor mount bolts, which reduce stress on the arm itself.

entries.title: Orqa QuadCore H7 — UART assignment quick reference
entries.symptom: Need to know which UARTs are available and what they're pre-assigned to on the QuadCore H7.
entries.cause: The H7 has 7 UARTs but several are pre-assigned. Knowing the layout prevents conflicts.
entries.fix: QuadCore H7 UART layout: UART1 = ESC telemetry (pre-assigned), UART2 = free (use for receiver), UART3 = free (GPS recommended), UART4 = free, UART5 = free, UART6 = VTX control recommended, UART7 = free. Betaflight target: ORQA_QUADCORE_H7. Enable Serial RX on UART2 for ELRS/Crossfire. Note: USB uses a virtual COM — doesn't occupy a UART number.

entries.title: Orqa 3030 Lite F405 — UART and motor output layout
entries.symptom: Setting up the 3030 Lite F405 and need UART and motor pad locations.
entries.cause: The F405 is a 30x30 stack FC — tighter layout than H7. 5 UARTs available.
entries.fix: 3030 Lite F405 layout: UART1 = ESC telemetry, UART2 = Serial RX (receiver), UART3 = GPS, UART4 = free (VTX or other), UART6 = free. Motor outputs M1–M4 on corners, M5–M6 on dedicated pads. Betaflight target: ORQA_3030LITE_F405. Stack with 3030 70A ESC using included mounting hardware — signal pads align with ESC connector.

entries.title: Orqa WingCore H7 — PX4/ArduPilot setup for fixed-wing
entries.symptom: Setting up WingCore H7 for a fixed-wing or VTOL build. Need to know port assignments and firmware.
entries.cause: WingCore H7 is designed for PX4/ArduPilot, not Betaflight. Different pinout philosophy than FPV FCs.
entries.fix: WingCore H7 setup: 1) Flash PX4 or ArduPilot — NOT Betaflight. 2) 8 JST-GH ports: GPS1 (UART4), GPS2 (UART5), TELEM1 (UART2 — GCS link), TELEM2 (UART3), RC input (UART6), I2C1, CAN1, CAN2. 3) 14 PWM outputs for servos/ESCs. 4) 160A current sensor onboard — configure in PX4 SENS_BATT_A_PER_V parameter. 5) Connect QGroundControl via USB for initial config.

entries.title: NDAA §848 compliance check fails for procured drone
symptoms: Procurement officer flags drone for NDAA §848 review
Platform not on Blue UAS cleared list
Federal grant reimbursement denied due to non-compliant equipment
causes: Platform manufactured by covered entity (DJI, Autel, SZ DJI, Yunneec, etc.)
Platform not reviewed under NDAA §848 by DIU
Misreading of Blue UAS Framework vs Blue UAS Cleared List distinction
diagnostics: Check DIU Blue UAS Cleared List at diu.mil/blue-uas-cleared-list
Verify NDAA §848 exclusion list — DJI, Autel Robotics, SZ DJI, Yuneec
Check FCC Covered List for additional restricted vendors
Confirm whether platform is Framework-listed (not yet cleared) vs Cleared
fixes: Replace with Blue UAS cleared platform (Skydio, Teal, Freefly Astro, etc.)
Request NDAA §848 waiver from agency legal counsel — rarely granted
For non-federal grants: check state/local funding body's specific NDAA requirement
Document justification memo using forge_manufacturer_status.json data

entries.title: DFR program can't get FCC waiver for 4.9GHz public safety band
symptoms: FCC license application for 4.9GHz denied or stalled
City IT department unfamiliar with public safety UAS spectrum requirements
Interference complaints from adjacent public safety users on 4.9GHz
causes: 4.9GHz is licensed band — requires FCC Part 90 license for public safety agencies
Application submitted through wrong FCC ULS form (should be FCC Form 601 Schedule H)
Frequency coordination not done with adjacent agencies
diagnostics: Confirm agency has existing Part 90 license or is eligible (police, fire, EMS)
Check ULS license database at fcc.gov/uls for existing agency licenses
Identify 4.9GHz channels: 4940–4990 MHz, 5 MHz channels, licensed per site
fixes: File FCC Form 601 Schedule H — Public Safety Pool, Part 90.1207
Use frequency coordinator (APCO, FCCA) for channel assignment
Alternatively use licensed 900MHz MANET (Doodle Labs, Silvus) — no FCC 4.9GHz needed
Contact Brent Williams at FCC Public Safety Bureau: 202-418-1160

entries.title: DFR drone fails AEL eligibility check for FEMA BRIC/HMGP grant
symptoms: Grant reviewer rejects drone line item as AEL-ineligible
AEL code 03OE-07-SUAS listed as ineligible for requested platform
causes: Platform not on DHS Authorized Equipment List (AEL) 03OE-07-SUAS
Platform on FCC Covered List — automatically AEL-ineligible
Grant submitted without NDAA §848 compliance documentation
diagnostics: Check DHS AEL at rkb.fema.gov — search '03OE-07-SUAS'
Verify platform against FCC Covered List (DJI/Autel/SZ DJI excluded)
Confirm subgrant agreement includes UAS equipment explicitly
fixes: Select platform from AEL-eligible Blue UAS cleared list
Include NDAA compliance memo with procurement documentation
For existing non-compliant purchases: request retroactive waiver through state FEMA office
Document with manufacturer's self-attestation letter referencing 47 CFR Part 2

entries.title: Remote ID module not broadcasting — COW application flagged
symptoms: FAA COW application rejected — Remote ID compliance not demonstrated
Remote ID app (FAA DroneZone) shows no broadcast from aircraft
Module broadcasting but not recognized by FAA SIMS system
causes: Module not on FAA-accepted means of compliance list (ASTM F3586-22)
Module installed but not configured for Standard Remote ID broadcast mode
Bluetooth/WiFi broadcast interference from other onboard electronics
Module firmware outdated — FAA updated accepted versions list
diagnostics: Check FAA accepted Remote ID declarations at faa.gov/uas/getting_started/remote_id
Scan for Remote ID broadcast using OpenDroneID app (Android)
Verify module FCC ID against accepted list
Check module SSID broadcast: should be 'FAA_RID_<serial>'
fixes: Update to FAA-accepted module: uAvionix ping1090i, DroneTag Mini, Dronetag BS
Configure Standard RID mode (not Limited RID) for broadcast
Mount module away from power distribution board — EMI interference common
For COW application: include Remote ID module FCC ID and declaration

entries.title: DFR program audit — Part 107 vs Part 91 COW record-keeping failure
symptoms: FAA audit finds missing maintenance records
Pilot logs don't capture DFR-specific mission data
Insurance claim denied due to inadequate incident documentation
causes: No structured maintenance tracking system in place
Pilots using personal logbooks not agency systems
COW requires monthly FAA reporting — agency not submitting
No pre-flight checklist documentation retained
diagnostics: Review COW conditions — most require monthly operational reports to FAA
Check pilot logs for currency: 3 takeoff/landings in 90 days for night ops
Verify maintenance records include battery cycle counts, motor hours, crash history
fixes: Implement structured logbook: FlightLogger, Kittyhawk, or DroneDeploy for agencies
Set up monthly FAA reporting cadence — email to 9-ator-hq-sosc@faa.gov
Create pre-flight checklist template (use DroneClear waiver builder SOP template)
Use Tooth (Wingman) for tamper-evident parameter change audit trail
